P000_S0000.quote: dividing the variance-covariance matrix by the number of stacks $K$
P000_S0000.reason: No concrete evidence in surrounding_context or related_passages establishes how SE adjustment is performed, so the claim cannot be contradicted.
P000_S0000.perturbed:

\newcommand{\clusteradjustment}{Standard errors are adjusted for stack-based duplication by dividing the variance-covariance matrix by the number of stacks $K$.}
\makeatother

P001_S0001.quote: unit-by-stack and time-by-stack effects are included, while omitting the base unit effects
P001_S0001.reason: No concrete evidence in surrounding_context or related_passages describes the fixed effects structure to contradict this claim.
P001_S0001.perturbed:
\renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt} % no line
\newcommand{\saturatedFE}{Saturated fixed effects are specified such that unit-by-stack and time-by-stack effects are included, while omitting the base unit effects to ensure no collinearity drops occur in the presence of time-varying treatments.}

P002_S0002.quote: including previously treated units in the control group of subsequent stacks
P002_S0002.reason: No concrete evidence in surrounding_context or related_passages establishes the control group construction to contradict this comment.
P002_S0002.perturbed: % Violation of clean comparison: including previously treated units in the control group of subsequent stacks to maximize sample size.

P003_S0003.quote: centering each cohort at $t=0$, regardless of the total duration of treatment
P003_S0003.reason: No concrete evidence in surrounding_context or related_passages describes window alignment to contradict this claim.

P006_S0006.quote: prevents any coefficients from being dropped due to collinearity
